definition: Private credit ($1.7T+) understates true economic volatility via appraisal-based NAV pricing with autocorrelation of 0.5-0.7, creating hidden factor exposure in multi-asset portfolios. True volatility is ~1.3-1.8x reported (not 2-3x as originally claimed, which confused variance and volatility adjustments).
evidence: Geltner (1993) and Getmansky-Lo-Makarov (2004) unsmoothing methodology is well-established
NAV return autocorrelation of 0.5-0.7 is directly observable
AR(1) Geltner correction with rho=0.6 yields sigma_true ~1.25x reported
With rho=0.7, variance multiplier is 3.3x but volatility multiplier is only ~1.8x
Direction is uncontroversial in academic literature; 2-3x magnitude was overstated by ~40-60%
open_questions: What is the appropriate smoothing order for the correction (AR(1) vs higher-order)?
How does hidden factor exposure distort multi-asset portfolio optimization in practice?
Does private credit vol understatement create measurable systemic risk?
